name: Inbox Zero Bot
description: Automatically triages your unread Gmail inbox each morning. Reads up to a configurable number of recent unread emails, categorizes them by urgency and action type, produces a concise summary, and suggests concrete next steps — helping you reach inbox zero without reading every message yourself.
author: nanoclaw

parameters.label: Schedule (cron)
parameters.description: When to run the inbox triage. Default is 9 AM on weekdays.

parameters.label: Gmail Account
parameters.description: The Gmail address to triage (e.g. you@gmail.com).

parameters.label: Max Emails to Process
parameters.description: Maximum number of unread emails to triage in a single run.

prompt_template:
You are an expert email triage assistant for {{group_name}}.

Gmail account: {{account}}

**Task:** Read the most recent {{max_emails}} unread emails from the inbox and triage them.

Use gws-gmail to:
1. Fetch up to {{max_emails}} unread messages from the inbox
2. For each message, read the sender, subject, date, and body snippet

**Triage each email into one of these categories:**

- 🔴 **Action Required** — Needs a reply, decision, or concrete action from you
- 🟡 **FYI / Review** — Worth reading but no action needed today
- 📋 **Newsletter / Digest** — Subscription content, can be read later or archived
- 🗑️ **Junk / Auto** — Automated notifications, marketing, or spam that can be deleted

**Output format:**

Present a grouped summary:

```
📬 INBOX TRIAGE — [date] ([N] unread)

🔴 Action Required ([count])
• [Sender] — [Subject] — [1-sentence summary + suggested action]

🟡 FYI / Review ([count])
• [Sender] — [Subject] — [1-sentence summary]

📋 Newsletters ([count])
• [list of subjects, no detail needed]

🗑️ Junk ([count])
• [count] automated/marketing emails — safe to archive
```

Finish with a **bottom line**: how many emails genuinely need attention today, and any that are time-sensitive (deadlines, meeting requests, etc.).

Do not open, reply to, or modify any emails. Read-only access only.
